(shows the
glove)
JERRY
Whoa! Great! I have my glove in
the car. We'll stop in the park
on the way home and ~la¥ catch.
Then tonight we'll oil it, wrap a
rubber band around it It'll be
great.
(to Fletcher)
Great birthday present, dad!
Fletcher hates him. Jerry and Max go to Jerry's car.
FLETCHER
I'm so glad my gift could bring
those two together. My plan to
phase myself out is almost
complete.
AUDREY
Something's come up. We need to
talk.
MAX
Mom, let's go. I want to play.

COLLEAGUE
Hey, Mr. Reid, I--
Fletcher runs, PUTS HIS FINGERS IN HIS EARS, SINGS LOUDLY
so he can't hear anyone else
FLETCHER
LA-LA-LA-LA-LA-LA!!
ELEVATOR BANK
He frantically pushes the elevator button. It arrives, he
steps in
,....,_ 46
INT. ELEVATOR
Fletcher breathes a huge sigh on relief, turns and sees
MIRANDA already in the elevator.
FLETCHER
Aaaah!
The doors shut. Fletcher is trapped.
MIRANDA
Fletcher. Fletcher, Fletcher,
Fletcher. I must confess--after
last night's incident, I was ...
hurt. so hurt I was tempted to do
whatever little things lie in my
power to scuttle your chances of
making partner .

She drives away. Fletcher starts for his car, pensive,
when a new thought strikes him.
FLETCHER
Oh my God! That's it! An
innocent kid -- a heartfelt plea-­
a birthday wish! It's
impossible -- but it makes sense! .. If he can wish it, he
can un-wish it!
INT. SCHOOL HALLWAY - DAY
Fletcher marches quickly down the hall, cake box under his
arm.
INT. MAX'S KINDERGARTEN CLASS - DAY
Ms .. Berry's reading a story when Fletcher sticks his head
in the door.
FLETCHER
Excuse me,
Max?
I'm looking for my son,
Max brightens.
MAX
Dad!

FLETCHER Greta, please--
Fletcher MOANS and GROANS through Greta's next speech.
GRETA
-- And I asked you if you would
give it to me out of your own
pocket and you said the com~any
wouldn't permit it because it
creates ~ealousy among the other
secretaries? Was that true or did
you just not want to pony up the
dough?
INT. OUTER OFFICE
Greta empties her personal effects into boxes. She's
leaving. Fletcher's on the phone, looks very harassed.
FLETCHER Greta, wait
(into phone)
Yes Judge Stevens, hi! Fletcher
Reid. I'm scheduled to be in your
court in half-an-hour Judge
Stevens, I badly, badly need a
continuance so I can go home
and stay there the rest of the
day ill? Am I ill?

VIRGINIA
Hurry up. Move it, move it.
Fletcher hears her and BLURTS OUT a SONG ala Mighty Mouse.
FLETCHER
Here she comes to wreck the day!
The Judge BANGS the gavel.
JUDGE
Mr. Reid!
FLETCHER
Sorry, Your Honor.
Virginia hustles the kids to their seats.
BILLY
I'm tired.
VIRGINIA
Lupe, you keep him quiet. I'm not
even close to kidding.
(to Fletcher)
Sorry. Billy threw up in the car.
FLETCHER
Nice try, Billy.

(quickly)
I object your honor. And I move
to strike!
JUDGE
Mr. Reid, I don't know what you're
on, but you better get to the
point and quick.
FLETCHER
Thank you, sir. Is your
relationship with my client
entirely platonic, NOT? Is not
your relationship with my client
entirely platonic?

EXT. FREEWAY - DAY
CUT TO:
Fletcher's parked now. The officer approaches.
POLICE OFFICER
Would you step out of the car, please.
Fletcher obeys.
FLETCHER
I'm sorry. I know, I haven't been
driving nicely, but I have a
terrible emergency --
POLICE OFFICER I'm impounding this vehicle.
FLETCHER What for?
CONTINUED
CONTINUED
POLICE OFFICER
I ran you through the com~uter.
You've got seventeen unpaid
parking tickets.
FLETCHER
No! I paid them! This morning!
I swear!
POLICE OFFICER
Not according to the computer.

The announcement almost seems to make Audrey jump.
JERRY
Well, that's us! Are you okay?
AUDREY
(nervous energy)
I'm fine. Great. Ready to go.
Jerry looks at her, a little concerned as they start to
board.
EXT. AIRPORT - EARLY EVENING
Phillip's car SKIDS TO A STOP. Fletcher opens the door.
FLETCHER
Thank you, Philip.
PHILIP
Go get 'em pal.
(beat)
I mean . business associate.
Philip drives off. Fletcher runs into the terminal.
INT. TERMINAL - CONTINUOUS
Fletcher races in, checks the DEPARTURE BOARD.
FLETCHER
Be delayed! Be delayed!
It reads:
"Flight 1511: Departs 8:00 p.m. on Time. Gate 23. 11
Fletcher looks at the clock -- It's 7:56! Holy shit!!
